futures_this_morning.driver: US-Iran ceasefire over Strait of Hormuz; tech bounce after last week's -4.6% Nasdaq loss

rates.outlook: possible hike in 2026; dot plot pushed cuts to 2027-2028

events: Wed Jul 1 9:30am ET — Fed Chair Warsh speaks (Europe; any hawkish tone moves rates)
Thu Jul 2 8:30am ET — NFP jobs report (June, consensus +172k; released one day early for July 4 holiday)
geopolitical: US-Iran ceasefire announced over weekend; Strait of Hormuz reopening; situation fluid
note: Market is in a technical uptrend (SPY 6.6% above 200-day SMA) but is expensive by any valuation measure. The Fed is on hold with a bias toward hikes, not cuts. Core PCE is running at 3.4%, well above target. A big jobs number Thursday could push rate-hike odds higher. We hold no positions and have full budget available, but the cautious posture stands: demand a meaningful margin of safety before deploying cash.
